arts.ttl: Arsenal flop celebrates New Year's Eve with adult film star girlfriend Teanna Trump
arts.sum: Expensive Arsenal flop Nicolas Pepe celebrated 2024 turning into 2025 by spending the evening with his partner, adult film star Teanna Trump, who have gone public with their relationship

arts.ttl: How Arsenal must overhaul squad: Who Gunners should keep and sell ahead of big transfer year
arts.sum: Arsenal need to offload some deadwood and strengthen in key areas

arts.ttl: Full Arsenal squad available vs Brentford as four players ruled out after Mikel Arteta update
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta's side make the trip to the Gtech Community Stadium on New Year's Day looking to secure another precious three points in their quest to win the league title. Riccardo Calafiori is a contender to start for Arsenal, though, after being restricted to just three appearances across the last ten Premier League matches due to a knee injury.

arts.ttl: Free agent Dani Olmo: What next for Barcelona as Arsenal, Man Utd circle?
arts.sum: Dani Olmo's registration with LaLiga has officially expired, meaning that he is now free to terminate his Barcelona contract. The club had hoped that selling off their VIP seats of the new Camp Nou to investors in Qatar and Dubai would enable them to secure the eligibility of Olmo prior to the December 31 deadline, but LaLiga announced on Tuesday that the Catalans had failed to achieve this.

arts.ttl: Bukayo Saka sends four-word message to Arsenal fans after 'difficult' injury update
arts.sum: The England international is now expected to be in recovery from the injury for several weeks as Arsenal continue their quest to beat the likes of Liverpool, Chelsea and Manchester City to the Premier League title. Arsenal return to Premier League action this evening as they travel to face Brentford on New Year's Day. It will be several more weeks until Saka is fit enough to feature for the Gunners again, with Arteta confirming last week that it will be at least two months on the sidelines for the Arsenal No.7.

arts.ttl: Arsenal may be forced into brutal Raheem Sterling decision amid Premier League loan rules
arts.sum: Arsenal have no plans to terminate Raheem Sterling's loan spell early, despite the winger's limited game time since his deadline day switch to the Emirates Stadium from Chelsea. Premier League regulations allow only two players on loan from domestic competitors, and Sterling was joined by Bournemouth goalkeeper Neto at the Emirates Stadium.

arts.ttl: Brentford vs Arsenal TV details - channel, live stream and kick-off time
arts.sum: Yes, TNT Sports have chosen Brentford vs Arsenal as part of their coverage for this round of Premier League fixtures. Here, football.london takes a look at the TV info needed to be able to follow Brentford vs Arsenal in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Arsenal announce first January transfer as 20-year-old joins League One side on permanent deal
arts.sum: Arsenal confirm their first exit of the January transfer window, as 20-year-old right-back Josh Robinson leaves for Wigan Athletic on a permanent deal.

arts.ttl: Exclusive: How Alexander Isak privately feels about January transfer amid Arsenal and Chelsea links
arts.sum: Alexander Isak has now made his decision 🚨

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta fires strong warning to Liverpool over Premier League title race
arts.sum: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta fires a warning to Liverpool over their Premier League title hopes.
